uv run python - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
import pandas as pd, re
for p in [Path('results/ablation_v3_features.csv'),Path('results/v3_features.csv')]:
    df=pd.read_csv(p, nrows=1)
    cols=list(df.columns)
    pats=['_ro','_pi','_ya','roll','pitch','yaw','FreeAcc','free','acc','gyr','gm','am','trn_','fc_','ev_']
    print('\nFILE',p,'ncols',len(cols))
    for pat in pats:
        m=[c for c in cols if pat.lower() in c.lower()]
        print(f'{pat}: {len(m)} sample={m[:12]}')
    prefixes={}
    for c in cols:
        pref=c.split('_')[0]
        prefixes[pref]=prefixes.get(pref,0)+1
    print('top prefixes', sorted(prefixes.items(), key=lambda x:x[1], reverse=True)[:25])
PY

bash ~/.claude/skills/pd-imu-100x-researcher/scripts/tri_consult.sh "$(cat <<'PROMPT_EOF'
[Context: WearGait-PD UPDRS-III T1 regression (items 9-14 sum), N=93 PD subjects, LOOCV, 3 seeds {42,1337,7}.]
[Canonical floor: iter12-honest CCC=0.6550. Strongest candidate: iter34 hybrid CCC=0.7366.]
[Iter34 architecture: Stage-1 Ridge(α=1.0) on 9 clinical features. Stage-2 = 8-item RegressorChain {LGB, XGB-hist, ExtraTrees} on K=500 LGB-importance-selected V2 features (1751 handcrafted cols: <Sensor>_<channel>_<statistic>). Fold-local K=500 selection shared across base learners. T1 = sum(item predictions 9-14).]
[Wall data points (11 negatives at N=93/94/98): F19/F44/F45/F48/F51 wide feature additions (K=500 absorption), F53 per-item composition (variance compounding), F54 single-loop hybrid leaky, F56 nested k=19 meta-stack (curse-of-dim), F58 convex k=1 blend (LOOCV residual scale mismatch), F59 Stage-1 widening + forced-inclusion (partial-r collapse), F61 sample-weight retrain + post-hoc calibration (regression-to-mean), F63 SOTA AutoML/shape-features (algo-class wall), F35-A ordinal cum-link loss family (MSE-on-residuals already near-optimal for CCC-of-summed-endpoint), F35-B Bayesian 2-factor LKJ SKIPPED (per-subject latent vanishes at LOOCV). Slot C phase-locked features BLOCKED on raw data.]
[Council lesson: FWER n=5, strict gate frac>0 ≥ 0.99 per slot. Marginal credibility of probe N+1 can be NEGATIVE under Bonferroni.]
[User banned for Slot D: NO chain+ensemble axis (iter34 exhausts), NO per-subject latents (slot B critique), NO wide feature additions (K=500 absorption wall), NO frozen encoders (F41/F45/F51), NO composite blends (F53/F56/F58), NO sample-weight retrain (F61), NO SOTA AutoML/shape heads (F63).]

We need THREE concrete architectural candidates ranked by expected lift over iter34 0.7366:

A. Anatomical Mixture-of-Experts × per-item routing: Partition V2 by sensor anatomy into E_axial={Forehead,Xiphoid,LowerBack}, E_lower={L/R_DorsalFoot,L/R_LatShank,L/R_Thigh}, E_upper={L/R_Wrist,L/R_UpArm}, E_residual. Per fold, independent Stage-2 LGB on each expert (K≈125 via fold-local LGB-importance), per item in {9,10,11,12,13,14,15,18}. At prediction, combine expert outputs via fold-locally-fit per-item Ridge gate. Final T1 = sum of expert-weighted item predictions.

B. Per-item adaptive K: Same chain×ensemble as iter34, but K_item is selected via inner CV per item per fold from {100,250,500,1000}. The global K=500 forced on all items may oversaturate item-13 (posture, needs few features) while undersaturating item-10 (gait, needs many).

C. Tree-distance-based fold-local item-similarity weights: Instead of RegressorChain's fixed-order chain link, compute pairwise item residual-cosine in train fold; train LGB on each item with its top-3 most-similar items' residuals injected as features.

For EACH candidate, answer:
1. Closest existing wall data point in mechanism that predicts failure.
2. Expected ΔCCC vs iter34 (point estimate + 80% CI).
3. P(strict gate frac>0 ≥ 0.99 vs iter34 on N=93 paired-bootstrap).
4. Which observable SHOULD specifically move if mechanism is real (per-item lift profile).

CRITICAL: Identify any way each candidate IS isomorphic to iter34's chain×ensemble or F70's mechanism. A 3-of-3 dissent on (candidate ≡ existing arch under reparameterization) → SKIP slot.

Honestly assess: is there a 4th candidate orthogonal to all 3 + the wall, that NEITHER changes loss family (slot A failed) NOR introduces per-subject latents (slot B failed)? If the wall is genuinely closed at this N, say so explicitly.

Be DIRECT. If your honest prior is P(strict gate)<0.20 for all 3, say so plainly and recommend SKIP.

Format: RANK ORDER: [letter, letter, letter]
PER-CANDIDATE BRIEF: A: ... B: ... C: ...
4TH CANDIDATE (if any): ...
SKIP RECOMMENDATION (yes/no, with reasoning):
EXPECTED OUTCOME of slot D execution under your top-1: ...
PROMPT_EOF
)"

Goal: break or honestly close the corrected T3 MDS-UPDRS Part III CCC ceiling. Current canonical T3 is iter47 valid-range LOOCV CCC 0.3784, N=95; LOSO two-way 0.150. Residual audits show tail compression: residual corr(true severity) -0.777; unobservable_non_gait domain residual r -0.800; top residual items are item6 pronation/supination r -0.571, item4 finger tapping r -0.528, item5 hand movements r -0.469, item3 rigidity r -0.460. Observable gait/balance items 7-14 have weaker residual links. Existing blocker audit says zero local WearGait-only model actions remain; public external routes FoG-STAR, COPS, TLVMC/DeFOG, PDFE, Parkinson@Home have already been tested/closed as external-validity only or hard-stopped. Gated priority routes are PPMI/Verily, PPP/PD-VME, WATCH-PD, CNS Portugal/Lobo, Hssayeni/MJFF, ICICLE-GAIT. New public source fetches confirm PPMI/Verily longitudinal wrist data with UPDRS within 90 days and ICICLE-GAIT lower-back 7-day MDS-UPDRS Part III prediction. I am considering implementing a deterministic audit script that (1) computes a diagnostic T1-prediction-to-T3 scaffold from existing saved OOF predictions, (2) scores external routes by access readiness and protocol coverage of the unobservable residual domains, and (3) emits a ranked opportunity ledger with no preregistration/no model-promotion.
